constraints: Strict adherence to UK GDPR and the Data Protection Act 2018.
Mandatory alignment with Government Security Classifications (GSC) for all data assets.
Limited availability of specialized AI and data governance subject matter experts.
introduction: This process establishes a consistent, secure, and ethical framework for AI and data management within the Ministry of Justice (MoJ) to mitigate risk and improve public service outcomes. This framework is designed to ensure that all AI-driven initiatives are developed and deployed with the highest standards of integrity, transparency, and accountability. By standardizing governance procedures, the MoJ aims to foster public trust and ensure that data-driven decisions are both legally compliant and ethically sound.
governance_requirements: Maintenance of a central AI model inventory
process_triggers: Initiation of a new digital project or a significant change to an existing data-driven system.
assumptions: All MoJ projects have access to the centralized data catalog and governance toolkit.
Project teams have sufficient technical capability to implement data quality controls.
Central governance boards meet at a frequency that does not create delivery bottlenecks.
scope: All internal MoJ digital projects and third-party vendor solutions processing justice-related data or utilizing AI.

critical_failure_factors.description: Overly bureaucratic approval processes leading to 'Shadow AI'.
critical_failure_factors.name: Bureaucratic Bottlenecks

critical_failure_factors.description: Lack of clear data ownership at the project level.
critical_failure_factors.name: Undefined Data Ownership

critical_failure_factors.description: Inadequate funding for automated data quality tooling.
critical_failure_factors.name: Resource Constraints

responsibilities: Accountable for the enterprise framework and final policy decisions.
Responsible for ensuring project-level compliance with the standard.
Providing oversight on data protection and legal risks.
Developing AI models and implementing technical quality controls.

process_steps.purpose: To ensure visibility of all AI/Data projects within the MoJ.
process_steps.description: Formal intent to use AI/Data is logged with the Data Office. This step ensures that the central governance body is aware of all emerging AI projects, allowing for early intervention and guidance on compliance requirements.
success_criteria: Project ID issued and logged in central inventory.

process_steps.purpose: To ensure data is handled according to its sensitivity.
process_steps.description: Identification and tagging of data assets according to Government Security Classifications (GSC). This step is critical for ensuring that data is handled with the appropriate level of security and that privacy controls are applied based on the sensitivity of the information.
success_criteria: 100% of project data assets classified and inventoried.

process_steps.scope: Privacy and ethical impact of the AI system.
process_steps.purpose: To identify and mitigate privacy and ethical risks.
process_steps.description: Conduct Data Protection Impact Assessments (DPIAs) and AI ethical risk assessments. This phase involves a deep dive into the potential risks associated with data processing and AI model behavior, ensuring that mitigation strategies are integrated into the project design.
success_criteria: DPIA and Ethical Assessment signed off.

process_steps.scope: Technical implementation of the AI solution.
process_steps.description: Implementation of technical controls for security, privacy, and quality. This step translates the risk mitigation plan into actionable technical safeguards, such as encryption, access controls, and automated data quality checks.
success_criteria: All identified risks have corresponding technical controls.
process_steps.purpose: To apply necessary safeguards to the data and AI model.

process_steps.scope: Verification of security and quality controls.
process_steps.description: Independent verification of data quality and security posture. An objective review is conducted to ensure that all implemented controls are functioning as intended and that the system meets the MoJ's rigorous security and quality standards.
process_steps.purpose: To provide assurance that controls are effective.
To obtain formal organizational approval for operation.
process_steps.description: Presentation of evidence to the MoJ Governance Board for approval. This final gate involves a comprehensive review of all project documentation and audit results to grant formal authorization for the system to go live.

process_steps.purpose: To ensure continued accuracy and safety of the AI system.
success_criteria: Model performance remains within defined thresholds.
process_steps.description: Ongoing tracking of model performance and data drift. Once operational, the system is continuously monitored to detect any degradation in performance or shifts in data patterns that could impact the accuracy or fairness of the AI model.

change_management.versioning_rules: Semantic versioning for models and data schemas; all changes logged in central inventory.
change_management.change_request_process: Mandatory re-assessment for any updates to AI model logic or underlying data schemas.

risks_and_controls.control: Mandatory human-in-the-loop validation for high-impact AI decisions.
risks_and_controls.risk: Bias or inaccuracy in AI models affecting judicial outcomes.

risks_and_controls.control: Encryption at rest/transit and automated classification labelling.
risks_and_controls.risk: Unauthorized access or leakage of sensitive person-identifiable information (PII).

metrics.description: Percentage of projects passing governance gates.
metrics.name: Compliance Rate

metrics.description: Number of data security incidents involving AI systems.
metrics.name: Security Incidents

metrics.description: Average lead time for governance approval cycles.
metrics.name: Approval Lead Time

purpose: To establish a consistent, secure, and ethical framework for AI and data management within the Ministry of Justice to mitigate risk and improve public service outcomes.
process_name: MoJ AI and Data Governance Framework

system_requirements.details: Enterprise Data Catalog (e.g., Collibra) and AI Governance Platform.
system_requirements.name: Governance Platforms

system_requirements.details: Secure Data Environments (SDE) and Automated Data Profiling tools.
system_requirements.name: Data Infrastructure

process_end_conditions: Issuance of a formal Authorization to Operate (AtO) or system decommissioning.

critical_success_factors.description: Executive mandate from MoJ leadership for framework adherence.
critical_success_factors.name: Executive Mandate

critical_success_factors.description: Integrated automated scanning for data classification and quality.
critical_success_factors.name: Automated Tooling

critical_success_factors.description: Clear communication of ethical AI principles across the department.
critical_success_factors.name: Ethical Alignment
